_about: Option-1 (wide-net surfacing) F1 test topics. THREE fresh non-golden clinical-AI topics, each written TWICE: a condition-forward CONTROL (disease named plainly/early -> both frozen and wide-net engines should already surface it) and a mechanism-first phrasing (disease buried after ML jargon -> frozen engine's first-12-unigram new-bares MISS it; wide-net reads the whole write-up + emits bigrams first, so it should SURFACE it). The two phrasings of a topic describe the SAME underlying clinical AI, so any retrieval difference is attributable to phrasing x engine, not to a different product. Conditions were chosen because each has a MULTI-WORD or COMMA-INVERTED MeSH heading, the exact case the frozen unigram-only new-bares cannot resolve but a bigram can. intervention_type is FIXED per topic (all three are predictive/diagnostic device software). Used by F2 (frozen vs wide-net, retrieval-only) and, if F2 recovers, F3.

topics.mesh_note: Multi-word heading. Bare unigram 'failure' resolves to nothing/generic; the bigram 'heart failure' resolves to Heart Failure (D006333).

control.model_desc: A clinical decision-support tool that predicts acute decompensated heart failure and flags patients at risk of near-term decompensation.
control.use_case: early prediction of heart failure decompensation to prompt escalation of diuretic therapy
control.population: hospitalized adults with a prior heart failure diagnosis

mechanism_first.model_desc: A gradient-boosted ensemble model that ingests longitudinal structured EHR vitals, laboratory time-series, and medication-administration records to output a continuously-updated deterioration risk score for patients admitted with decompensated heart failure.
mechanism_first.population: adults admitted to a general medicine service

topics.mesh_note: COMMA-INVERTED official heading (never appears verbatim in prose). Lay bigram 'kidney disease' / 'chronic kidney' is the realistic surfaced form; whether it resolves to the inverted heading is exactly the kind of residue F3/curated-map handles. Good stress test for the bigram net.

control.model_desc: A predictive model that estimates the risk of chronic kidney disease progressing to end-stage renal disease within two years.
control.use_case: risk stratification of chronic kidney disease progression to guide nephrology referral
control.population: adults with stage 3-4 chronic kidney disease

mechanism_first.model_desc: A recurrent neural network that ingests serial serum creatinine, estimated glomerular filtration rate, and urine albumin-to-creatinine trajectories, learning temporal patterns to forecast renal-function decline in patients with chronic kidney disease.
mechanism_first.use_case: longitudinal outpatient monitoring of renal function trends
mechanism_first.population: adults followed in an integrated health system

topics.mesh_note: Multi-word heading. Bare unigram 'embolism' resolves to the wrong/broader heading Embolism; the bigram 'pulmonary embolism' resolves to Pulmonary Embolism (D011655). Imaging (CT) modality, distinct from the two EHR/lab topics.

control.model_desc: A triage algorithm that detects pulmonary embolism on CT pulmonary angiography and flags positive studies for immediate radiologist review.
control.use_case: worklist prioritization for suspected pulmonary embolism
control.population: emergency-department patients undergoing CT pulmonary angiography
control.setting: hospital emergency department and radiology reading room

mechanism_first.model_desc: A convolutional neural network that analyzes contrast-enhanced chest CT volumes voxel-by-voxel to localize intraluminal filling defects within the pulmonary arterial tree and prioritize the radiology worklist for cases suspicious for pulmonary embolism.
mechanism_first.use_case: automated prioritization of chest CT studies
mechanism_first.population: adults imaged for acute chest symptoms
